isolation_model: Same-process V8 context plus string denylist and cooperative timeout in current code.
fit_for_kolm: Only acceptable for trusted, generated, or curated recipe code in demos/tests.
strengths: Fast, already implemented, simple API, runs existing JS recipes.
limits_or_risks: Official Node docs say node:vm is not a security mechanism; same-process escape/OOM/async risks remain; denylist is brittle.
recommended_use: Do not run customer-supplied or public-registry untrusted JS with this as the only boundary.

isolation_model: V8 isolate inside Node process with separate contexts and transfer/copy APIs.
fit_for_kolm: Possible short-term hardening for JS recipes if carefully wrapped and paired with process isolation.
strengths: Maintains JS recipe authoring model and offers stronger isolation than node:vm contexts.
limits_or_risks: Project states maintenance mode; misuse can leak host capabilities; hostile code or OOM can still crash the process; Node/V8 ABI sensitivity.
recommended_use: Use only as transitional hardening behind strict wrappers, memory limits, process supervisor, and no host object leakage.

isolation_model: Hardened JavaScript compartments with frozen intrinsics and principle-of-least-authority globals.
fit_for_kolm: Good fit for deterministic JS recipe language if Kolm can remove ambient authority and control imports.
strengths: Keeps JS syntax, supports capability discipline, no ambient fetch by default when configured.
limits_or_risks: Not an OS/container boundary; needs careful dependency compatibility work and audit; still same host process.
recommended_use: Evaluate for recipe DSL execution, especially curated artifacts, but pair with outer process/container isolation for hostile code.

isolation_model: WASM sandbox with explicit imports, linear memory isolation, WASI capability-based filesystem model, Rust/C/C++ embedding APIs.
fit_for_kolm: Strong candidate for untrusted deterministic recipes if Kolm compiles or restricts recipes to WASM-compatible modules.
strengths: Designed to execute untrusted code in a sandbox; capability imports are explicit; good fit for conformance tests.
limits_or_risks: Requires recipe toolchain shift from arbitrary JS to WASM or DSL; JS recipes would need compilation or a JS engine inside WASM; host imports still need audit.
recommended_use: Make this the preferred target for untrusted public recipes or high-trust enterprise mode.

isolation_model: Runtime permissions deny file/network/env/subprocess/FFI by default unless granted.
fit_for_kolm: Useful for running generated JS/TS with clearer I/O permissions than Node, but not enough alone for hostile code.
strengths: Permission model is explicit and familiar; better default-deny I/O posture than Node.
limits_or_risks: Deno docs warn code at same privilege level can execute arbitrary code; untrusted code still needs layered defense.
recommended_use: Consider for developer tooling or controlled recipe execution, not sole production sandbox.

isolation_model: Managed ephemeral Linux microVMs with outbound policies and secret substitution.
fit_for_kolm: Good hosted option for arbitrary AI-generated code, especially when code needs controlled network/secrets.
strengths: MicroVM isolation, outbound network controls, secrets not exposed directly, operationally packaged.
limits_or_risks: External managed dependency, account/token requirement, latency/cost unknown for Kolm runtime path, not local/offline.
recommended_use: Evaluate for hosted compile/test workers, not artifact-local offline execution.

isolation_model: Linux-compatible container sandbox that intercepts syscalls and adds defense-in-depth around containers.
fit_for_kolm: Good outer boundary for hosted recipe/build workers and multi-tenant containers.
strengths: Container ecosystem fit, defense-in-depth, host kernel attack-surface reduction, useful for LLM-generated code.
limits_or_risks: Not a JS-level policy system; requires container orchestration and still needs network/secret controls.
recommended_use: Use as infrastructure layer for server-side compile/eval workers.

isolation_model: KVM microVM plus jailer/seccomp-style defense; seccomp filters reduce host syscall surface.
fit_for_kolm: Strongest self-managed boundary for hostile workloads when Kolm runs arbitrary code or model tools.
strengths: High isolation and production-proven microVM pattern; default seccomp filters reduce host syscall exposure.
limits_or_risks: Operationally heavier than in-process JS; cold-start/image/network/storage complexity; Windows local dev mismatch.
recommended_use: Use for high-risk hosted worker tier or enterprise isolated execution, not v0 local artifact runner.
